case_title: LUKE D. BRUGNARA VS. FRANCHISE TAX BOARD
cause_of_action: WRITS OF MANDATE OR PROH., CERTI., ETC./ADMIN. AGEN

docket_entries.description: REPLY TO PETITIONER'S OPPOS TO DEMUR TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE FILED BY RESPONDENT FRANCHISE TAX BOARD
MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-06-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. RESPONDENT FRANCHISE TAX BOARD'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION IS CONTINUED TO JULY 03, 2014 PER AGREEMENT OF ALL PARTIES. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.
NOTICE OF ORDER SUSTAINING DEMURRER FILED BY RESPONDENT FRANCHISE TAX BOARD
MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-03-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: NO ARGUMENT. COUNSEL SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR PETITIONER REQUESTS CONTINUATION OF HEARING. THE COURT DENIES REQUEST FOR CONTINUANCE. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. RESPONDENT FRANCHISE TAX BOARD'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. ART. XIII SEC. 32 OF THE CALIFORNIA CONSTITUTION PROVIDES: "NO LEGAL OR EQUITABLE PROCESS SHALL ISSUE IN ANY PROCEEDING IN ANY COURT AGAINST THIS STATE OR ANY OFFICER THEREOF TO PREVENT OR ENJOIN THE COLLECTION OF ANY TAX. AFTER PAYMENT OF A TAX CLAIMED TO BE ILLEGAL, AN ACTION MAY BE MAINTAINED TO RECOVER THE TAX PAID, WITH INTEREST, IN SUCH MANNER AS MAY BE PROVIDED BY THE LEGISLATURE." "A TAXPAYER MAY NOT GO INTO COURT AND OBTAIN ADJUDICATION OF THE VALIDITY OF A TAX WHICH IS DUE BUT NOT YET PAID. THE IMPORTANT PUBLIC POLICY BEHIND THIS CONSTITUTIONAL PROVISION 'IS TO ALLOW REVENUE COLLECTION TO CONTINUE DURING LITIGATION SO THAT ESSENTIAL PUBLIC SERVICES DEPENDENT ON THE FUNDS ARE NOT UNNECESSARILY INTERRUPTED.'" STATE BD. OF EQUALIZATION V. SUP. CRT. (1985) 39 CAL.3D 633, 638. IN THIS CASE, PETITIONER'S PETITION AND ATTACHMENTS FACIALLY DEMONSTRATE THAT HE HAS NOT PAID THE TAX. ACCORDINGLY, THE ACTION FAILS TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION. SEE CCP 430.10(E). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

calendar.matters:
DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Jul-03-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Kane, Esq., 415.982.1510, specially appearing for Petitioner Luke Brugnara.
David Lew, Deputy Attorney General, 510.622.2225, for respondent Franchise Tax Board.

RULING: No argument. Counsel specially appearing for petitioner requests continuation of hearing. The Court denies request for continuance. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT FRANCHISE TAX BOARD'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is sustained without leave to amend. Art. XIII sec. 32 of the California Constitution provides: "No legal or equitable process shall issue in any proceeding in any court against this State or any officer thereof to prevent or enjoin the collection of any tax. After payment of a tax claimed to be illegal, an action may be maintained to recover the tax paid, with interest, in such manner as may be provided by the Legislature." "A taxpayer may not go into court and obtain adjudication of the validity of a tax which is due but not yet paid. The important public policy behind this constitutional provision 'is to allow revenue collection to continue during litigation so that essential public services dependent on the funds are not unnecessarily interrupted.'" State Bd. of Equalization v. Sup. Crt. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 633, 638. In this case, petitioner's petition and attachments facially demonstrate that he has not paid the tax. Accordingly, the action fails to state a cause of action. See CCP 430.10(e). Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Jun-06-2014 Continued To Jul-03-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

RESPONDENT FRANCHISE TAX BOARD'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is continued to July 03, 2014 per agreement of all parties. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
